content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Found two policy-enforcement issues. Reviewed the branch diff against 2e54e84499c17f6d5cfafe87ff0d6ee5068770e8, ignored .ralph-burning runtime state as requested, and verified nix build passes.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5126-5184 still runs the unjournaled exact-title/rendered-description recovery path before the proposal threshold check at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5186-5210. A below-threshold propose_new_bead input can therefore add a dependency and return/record Created whenever a matching bead exists, instead of emitting propose_new_bead_pending with no mutation. That same recovery branch calls record_proposed_bead_created_event at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5174-5182, which omits the new threshold_count and existing_bead_lookup_ran metadata. Gate this recovery path behind the threshold, or require proof of a prior threshold-compliant creation, and emit the structured created event with the policy metadata.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: This violates the threshold enforcement and structured journal event acceptance criteria on an existing branch path not covered by the new tests.
2. src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4486-4488 loads parsimonious_bead_creation with project_id=None, and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5186-5220 uses that policy for enabled, proposal_threshold, and match threshold decisions. Project-level workflow.parsimonious_bead_creation settings are silently ignored, so a project can set enabled=false or proposal_threshold=3 and this handler will still use workspace/default values. Pass the project id into handle_propose_new_bead or otherwise load the same project-scoped EffectiveConfig used by reconciliation.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The new configuration is intended to control creation policy; ignoring project-scoped overrides can create or reroute beads contrary to the active run's config.
